He wrote to his wife Lydia on 11 March 1935'I had to lunch today the Fellowship candidate who seems much the cleverest on paper... He is excellent – there cannot be a shadow of doubt... Turing his name’ (see full text of the letter below).

So well-kept were the secrets of the place that the importance of the Bletchley work was not fully realized until the 1970s when the research documentation (held in the US and UK national archives) was made public.
